title: Smoothing over a spat
setting: You snapped at your partner this morning over something small. Now you're back home and want to make peace.

steps.speaker: Partnerin
steps.es: Du bisch hüt Morge ziemli schroff gsii zu mir.
steps.en: You were pretty short with me this morning.

options.es: Du häsch rächt, und es tuet mer leid.
options.feedback: Owning it straight away takes all the heat out. Excellent repair.
An explanation isn't an apology — lead with sorry, then the context.
Blame restarts the fight. This is the moment to de-escalate.

steps.speaker: Partnerin
steps.es: Es het mi würkli treffe. Ich han nöd gwüsst, was los isch.
steps.en: It really got to me. I didn't know what was going on.

options.es: Ich han Stress gha, aber das isch kei Entschuldigung gsii.
options.en: I was stressed, but that was no excuse.
options.feedback: Honest about the cause without using it as a shield. Mature and warm.

options.es: Tut mer leid, ich erkläre der's spöter.
options.feedback: Don't defer — she's open now. A short honest word matters more than timing.

options.es: Mach jetz nöd e so es Drama.
options.en: Don't make such a drama out of it now.
options.feedback: Dismissing her feelings after hurting them only deepens the rift.

steps.speaker: Partnerin
steps.es: Ich schätze, dass du das zuegisch.
steps.en: I appreciate that you're admitting it.

options.es: Mir sind es Team. Ich wott der nöd weh tue.
options.en: We're a team. I don't want to hurt you.
options.feedback: Reaffirms the bond. Exactly what closes a small wound.

options.es: Guet, denn isch ja alls wider okay.
options.en: Good, so everything's fine again then.
options.feedback: A little quick to wrap it up — let the reconciliation breathe a moment.

options.en: Can we just forget it now?
options.feedback: Understandable, but acknowledging beats rushing to move on.

steps.speaker: Partnerin
steps.es: Chum, mer mached öis en Tee und sitzed chli ane.
steps.en: Come on, let's make a tea and sit down for a bit.

options.en: I'd love to. I'll get the water.
options.feedback: Accepting the peace offering with a small gesture — lovely close.
Fine, but a warmer yes would meet her halfway after she reached out.
Turning down the olive branch undoes the whole apology.
